prompt: Build a settings form with sections for Profile (name input, bio textarea) and Preferences (theme toggle group for light/dark/system, notifications switch). Add a Save button at the bottom.
expected_output: A settings form using DUI components with correct import patterns and CSS layout.
expectations: Uses dui-input for name, dui-textarea for bio
Uses dui-toggle-group with dui-toggle children for theme selection (not dui-select or custom radios)
Uses dui-switch for notifications
Uses dui-button with variant='primary' for Save
Uses dui-field for form field label/description wiring
Imports components via side-effect imports (e.g. import '@deepfuture/dui-components/button')
Uses CSS flexbox or grid for layout, not DUI layout components
Uses design tokens (--space-*, --font-size-*) for spacing, not hardcoded px/rem

prompt: Create a delete confirmation dialog. When the user clicks a red 'Delete Account' button, show an alert dialog with a warning icon, title 'Are you sure?', description explaining the action is permanent, and Cancel/Delete buttons.
expected_output: An alert dialog with correct compound component hierarchy and danger styling.
expectations: Uses dui-alert-dialog (not dui-dialog) for destructive confirmation
Compound hierarchy: dui-alert-dialog > dui-alert-dialog-trigger + dui-alert-dialog-popup
Trigger contains dui-button with variant='danger'
Uses dui-icon with SVG in default slot for the warning icon
Cancel button uses dui-alert-dialog-close wrapper
Delete button uses variant='danger' and appropriate appearance
Uses ::part(root) or ::part(popup) for any visual effects beyond tokens
No !important in CSS

prompt: Build a dashboard layout with a collapsible sidebar on the left (logo, nav items for Dashboard, Analytics, Settings), breadcrumbs at the top of the main content area, and a data table showing recent orders with columns for Order ID, Customer, Amount, and Status (with colored badges).
expected_output: A full dashboard layout using sidebar, breadcrumb, data-table, and badge components.
expectations: Uses dui-sidebar-provider with dui-sidebar, dui-sidebar-content, dui-sidebar-menu, dui-sidebar-menu-item, dui-sidebar-menu-button
Uses dui-sidebar-inset for the main content area
Uses dui-breadcrumb with dui-breadcrumb-item, dui-breadcrumb-link, dui-breadcrumb-page
Uses dui-data-table with columns and data properties
Uses dui-badge for status column (via custom cell renderer)
Dark mode via data-theme='dark' on <html>, not class='dark' or manual overrides
Uses CSS flexbox/grid for page layout
No custom markup for separators, badges, or progress indicators

prompt: I have the DUI inspector available in my dev environment. I want to customize the sidebar to have a blue accent color and larger text. Walk me through the process.
expected_output: An inspector-first workflow discovering real token names before writing CSS.
expectations: Starts with __dui_inspect('dui-sidebar-menu-button') to discover available tokens
Uses __dui_inspect() to view the catalog and find sidebar-related components
Uses __dui_mutate.setComponentToken() or __dui_mutate.setToken() to prototype changes
Verifies changes with __dui_inspect() after mutation
Uses __dui_export() to get structured source changes
Does NOT guess token names — discovers them via inspector first
Acknowledges that the inspector shows the ground truth for available tokens

prompt: Build a notifications panel that opens from a bell icon button. It should show a list of notifications with timestamps, a badge showing unread count, and an empty state when there are none. Include a 'Mark all read' action.
expected_output: A popover-based notifications panel using DUI components throughout.
expectations: Uses dui-popover (not dui-dialog) for the notifications panel
Uses dui-badge for unread count indicator
Uses dui-icon for the bell icon
Uses dui-separator between notification items
Uses dui-scroll-area for the notification list if it could overflow
Uses dui-button for 'Mark all read' action
Does not use custom styled spans for badges or custom dividers for separators
Uses dui-spinner for loading state if applicable

prompt: Create a card grid showing 3 metric cards: Total Revenue, Active Users, and Conversion Rate. Each card should have a title, a large number, and a description showing the change from last period.
expected_output: A responsive card grid using dui-card-grid and dui-card components.
expectations: Uses dui-card-grid with columns='3' for responsive layout
Uses dui-card for each metric card
Uses dui-card slots: title, description, default for the metric value
Uses design tokens for typography and spacing, not hardcoded values
Does not build a custom card component from divs
Imports via side-effect imports
